changes.before: orchard walls and trimmed ditches insisted that someone still paid to keep this land neat. ... House Barov fed half a province and meant to keep doing it.
changes.after: orchard walls and trimmed ditches showed fresh labor and the money to keep this land neat. ... Barov grain fed half a province, and the crews here meant to keep it moving.

changes.before: She could almost hear Sylvanas asking ... if the house smiled and closed behind her.
changes.after: She could hear Sylvanas asking ... if the servants smiled and the gates closed behind her.

changes.before: enough Kirin Tor authority to make a noble house hesitate before it lied.
changes.after: enough Kirin Tor authority to make a noble family hesitate before lying.

changes.before: Good. Let the house react first.
changes.after: Good. Let the gate guards react first.

changes.before: A good horse, road-bred and steady around ward hum. ... That made it worse.
changes.after: road-bred and steady even with ward hum under her hooves.

changes.before: A man who signed things and let the world obey.
changes.after: A man used to signing orders and letting other people carry them out.

changes.before: Scholar's hands.
changes.after: Hands used to ink, glass, and live reagents.

changes.before: The house had been waiting for this knock, then. Maybe not for her specifically. For a version of her.
changes.after: Alexei and Jandice had been waiting for scrutiny, then. Perhaps for someone with questions and enough rank to force answers.

changes.before: Jandice glanced at her father, then back.
changes.after: Jandice checked her father for objection and found none.

changes.before: The oars entered the water with almost no sound.
changes.after: The oars entered the water with no sound.

changes.before: Caer Darrow rose out of the mist by pieces. First the seawall. Then towers. Then the keep itself.
changes.after: Caer Darrow rose out of the mist a piece at a time—the seawall first, then towers, then the keep itself.

changes.before: Curated, then.
changes.after: Someone had cleared the landing before she arrived.

changes.before: The strongest signature still did not come from the visible keep. It came from under it.
changes.after: The strongest signature came from under it.

changes.before: Routine use. It would have been easier if the place had wanted to leer.
changes.after: Routine was the point. Nobody bothered to hide what they were doing.

changes.before: Not identical handwriting. Kin handwriting. Lineage handwriting.
changes.after: Different hands, same lineage. The same training sat in every stroke.

changes.before: Real enough to trust. Dangerous enough to follow.
changes.after: Real work in those hands, and enough danger in it to pull Jaina farther in.

changes.before: The next rooms sharpened the method instead of broadening the horror.
changes.after: A preparation chamber held bodies on tables with linen over the face and numbered tags at the wrist.

changes.before: No chaos. No theatrical depravity. Only intellect with all the softness planed out of it.
changes.after: Every table was scrubbed, every tag tied straight, every instrument back in its place. The care in it felt worse than disorder.

changes.before: Not the layered hum of Dalaran's vaults. Pressure with teeth in it. ... The building admitted it by weight even while the tour denied it by direction.
changes.after: Pressure with teeth in it. ... The weight in the stone said more than Jandice's route did. She kept turning Jaina away from the pressure below.

changes.before: Recognition hunger. Not approval. Not charm.
changes.after: Jandice saw it and brightened at once, hungry for recognition.

changes.before: The room was comfortable to the point of insult.
changes.after: [deleted; left concrete room details only]

changes.before: Which meant the room was almost certainly listening.
changes.after: Wards in the room listened for voices all the same.

changes.before: Jaina stood still for a count of ten and let the chamber declare itself.
changes.after: Jaina stood still for a count of ten and read what the wards were doing.

changes.before: The same answer kept returning in different language. The body was not passive matter waiting for command from outside. The body learned.
changes.after: Page after page drove toward the same answer. The body learned the pattern, held it, repeated it.

changes.before: Not the whole truth.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: The building disliked being spoken through.
changes.after: The line fought her all the way.

changes.before: "Not imitation. Continuation."
changes.after: "The work continues ours."

changes.before: The room went back to itself. Dark lake. Warm walls. Hidden wards in the plaster.
changes.after: The room went back to dark lake beyond the glass, warm walls, and hidden wards in the plaster.

changes.before: Recognition. She wanted it badly enough for the need to show.
changes.after: Being understood hit Jandice hard enough to show.

changes.before: Dalaran saw the gate and declared the field forbidden so it would never have to learn what lay beyond it.
changes.after: The Council saw the gate and declared the field forbidden so they would never have to learn what lay beyond it.

changes.before: "Not entirely."
changes.after: "Partly below it."

changes.before: "I assumed that when the lake announced me."
changes.after: "I assumed that when the outer ward marked me."

changes.before: Jandice's eyes widened a fraction at the lack of hesitation. Then she smiled again.
changes.after: Jandice blinked once at the speed of her answer, then the pleased look returned.

changes.before: There should have been triumph in that. Jaina got none from it. Only the clean hard drop of initiative leaving her hands.
changes.after: There should have been triumph in that. Instead her stomach tightened. The next move belonged to someone else now.

changes.before: No screams. No chanting. Work.
changes.after: Only that sound, and someone putting it to use.

changes.before: Open route, Jaina thought. Trap all the same.
changes.after: The stair stayed open behind her. She tightened her grip on the ledger and followed Jandice lower.
